description:
You are a QA engineer preparing a quick summary of which test-case identifiers appear most often inside a raw log file so that the rest of your team can decide where to focus their effort.

    1. A directory already exists at  
       /home/user/test_env  

       Inside this directory there is one plain-text file called  
       /home/user/test_env/qa_cases.log  

    2. The job:  
       • Read qa_cases.log.  
       • Produce a frequency report that shows how many times each distinct line appears.  
       • Sort the output first by the descending occurrence count and, if two counts are equal, alphabetically (ascending) by the identifier text.  
       • Write the final, **fully sorted** result into a new file named  
         /home/user/test_env/freq_report.txt

    3. Output format (this will be checked by an automated test, so follow it exactly):  
       • One result per line.  
       • Each line consists of two fields separated by a single ASCII space:  
         a) a decimal integer count (no leading zeros)  
         b) the original line text (the test-case identifier)  
       • Do NOT add extra spaces or tabs.  
       • Do NOT prepend or append blank lines.  
       • Example line layout (count space identifier):  
         7 UI_Homepage_Load

    4. You may use only standard GNU/Linux command-line utilities that are normally present in a base distribution (e.g., sort, uniq, cat, etc.). No programming languages or third-party tools.

    5. When everything is finished, the directory /home/user/test_env should now contain **both** of these files:  
       - qa_cases.log  (original, unchanged)  
       - freq_report.txt (your newly created report)

    6. The automated test will:  
       • Verify that /home/user/test_env/freq_report.txt exists.  
       • Verify its contents match the format and exact ordering described above.  

    Do whatever intermediate commands you find useful, but only freq_report.txt’s final contents and existence will be evaluated.
truth:
Initial state:
    --------------------------------------------------
    File: /home/user/test_env/qa_cases.log
    Exact contents (10 lines, no trailing blank line):

    SEC_Login
    SEC_Login
    SEC_Logout
    UI_Homepage_Load
    UI_Homepage_Load
    UI_Homepage_Load
    API_GetUser
    API_GetUser
    API_GetUser
    API_GetUser

    --------------------------------------------------

    Required final state:
    A new file must exist:

    File: /home/user/test_env/freq_report.txt
    Exact contents (4 lines, no trailing blank line):

    4 API_GetUser
    3 UI_Homepage_Load
    2 SEC_Login
    1 SEC_Logout

    --------------------------------------------------
